title: QVHighlights: Detecting Moments and Highlights in Videos via Natural Language Queries
closest_relation_to_our_paper: QVHighlights is our Table 2 moment retrieval evaluation benchmark. Moment-DETR is the baseline transformer introduced with the dataset.
notes: 10K YouTube videos, moment + highlight detection annotations. Core evaluation benchmark for STITCH moment retrieval results.

filename: Generic Event Boundary Detection A Benchmark Challenge for Event Segmentation.pdf
title: Generic Event Boundary Detection: A Benchmark Challenge for Event Segmentation
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Kinetics-GEBD is our primary Table 1 GEBD evaluation benchmark. Defines the GEBD task and annotates generic event boundaries in Kinetics.
notes: Also includes TAPOS. STITCH evaluated on Rel.Dis. and F1@0.05 metrics from this benchmark.

filename: InternVideo2 Scaling Video Foundation Models for Multimodal Video Understanding.pdf
title: InternVideo2: Scaling Video Foundation Models for Multimodal Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: InternVideo2 is STITCH's frozen backbone. We reuse its per-clip embeddings for all downstream tasks without any fine-tuning.
notes: Multi-modal foundation model with strong temporal understanding. STITCH freezes it and applies KDE-based CPD on top.

filename: MLVU A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multi-Task Long Video Understanding.pdf
title: MLVU: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multi-Task Long Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: MLVU is one of our Table 3 VLM reasoning benchmarks. Tests multiple long-video understanding subtasks.
notes: Covers videos of varying lengths. STITCH improves frame selection for VLMs on MLVU.

filename: Video-MME The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis.pdf
title: Video-MME: The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Video-MME is one of our Table 3 VLM reasoning benchmarks. Tests video multimodal LLM capabilities.
notes: Short/medium/long video splits. STITCH used for frame selection on long-video split.

filename: LongVideoBench A Benchmark for Long-context Interleaved Video-Language Understanding.pdf
title: LongVideoBench: A Benchmark for Long-context Interleaved Video-Language Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: LongVideoBench is our third Table 3 benchmark. Interleaved video-language context for long-video QA.
notes: Validation split used in STITCH Table 3 results.

filename: Selective review of offline change point detection methods.pdf
title: Selective Review of Offline Change Point Detection Methods / ruptures library
closest_relation_to_our_paper: The ruptures Python library / kernel change-point detection is the algorithmic core of STITCH's temporal segmentation step.
notes: STITCH directly uses kernel-based CPD from this framework. Core citation for our CPD formulation.

filename: Optimal detection of changepoints with a linear computational cost.pdf
title: Optimal Detection of Changepoints with a Linear Computational Cost (PELT)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: PELT is the efficient change-point search algorithm underlying the ruptures library used in STITCH. Provides O(n) complexity for CPD.
notes: Key algorithmic reference for STITCH's linear-time chunking algorithm.

filename: Generic Event Boundary Detection via Denoising Diffusion.pdf
title: Generic Event Boundary Detection via Denoising Diffusion
closest_relation_to_our_paper: GEBD competitor on Kinetics-GEBD benchmark (Table 1). Uses diffusion model for event boundary detection.
notes: Supervised GEBD method; STITCH outperforms or is competitive without training.

filename: Rethinking Architecture Design for Efficient Generic Event Boundary Detection.pdf
title: Rethinking Architecture Design for Efficient Generic Event Boundary Detection
closest_relation_to_our_paper: GEBD competitor on Kinetics-GEBD benchmark (Table 1). Focused on architectural efficiency for GEBD.
notes: Supervised; direct comparison target for STITCH on GEBD.

filename: Online Generic Event Boundary Detection.pdf
title: Online Generic Event Boundary Detection
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Online GEBD competitor on Kinetics-GEBD (Table 1). Tests streaming/online setting.
notes: Supervised online setting. STITCH can also run online via streaming adaptation.

filename: Revisiting Kernel Temporal Segmentation as an Adaptive Tokenizer for Long-form Video Understanding.pdf
title: Revisiting Kernel Temporal Segmentation as an Adaptive Tokenizer for Long-form Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Most architecturally similar prior work. Also uses KTS-based adaptive tokenization on frozen features. STITCH extends with cosine kernel + adaptive partition + multi-task application.
notes: Directly related to STITCH's temporal segmentation strategy. Key differentiator: STITCH applies across GEBD/moment retrieval/VLM selection.

filename: Training-free Video Temporal Grounding using Large-scale Pre-trained Models.pdf
title: Training-free Video Temporal Grounding using Large-scale Pre-trained Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Training-free temporal grounding baseline; uses LLM+VLM pipeline (GPT-4+BLIP-2). Direct comparison on ActivityNet Captions and QVHighlights.
notes: Query-aware but training-free. Evaluated on same benchmarks as STITCH Table 2.

filename: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval from Frozen Vision-Language Models.pdf
title: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval from Frozen Vision-Language Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot frozen VLM approach for moment retrieval; no training, uses CLIP-like features. Direct comparison target.
notes: Training-free moment retrieval baseline. Compared in STITCH Table 2.

filename: Zero-shot Video Moment Retrieval via Off-the-shelf MLLMs.pdf
title: Zero-shot Video Moment Retrieval via Off-the-shelf MLLMs
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot training-free moment retrieval using off-the-shelf MLLMs. Direct baseline/comparison.
notes: Training-free; uses MLLMs like GPT-4V for temporal grounding. STITCH compared against this.

title: QD-DETR: Query-Dependent Video Representation for Moment Retrieval and Highlight Detection
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised moment retrieval + highlight detection on QVHighlights. Competitor baseline in Table 2.
notes: Query-conditioned DETR architecture. Strong supervised baseline that STITCH (training-free) compares against.

title: MovieChat: From Dense Token to Sparse Memory for Long Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video understanding via sliding window + cosine similarity token merging into short/long-term memory. Adjacent to STITCH's chunking approach.
notes: Uses cosine similarity for token merging, conceptually similar to STITCH's cosine kernel CPD.

filename: AdaReTaKe Adaptive Redundancy Reduction to Perceive Longer for Video-Language Models.pdf
title: AdaReTaKe: Adaptive Redundancy Reduction to Perceive Longer for Video-Language Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Adaptive token reduction for long video VLMs; training-free redundancy reduction. Adjacent to STITCH App 3.
notes: Frame selection/compression for long video. Supporting context for STITCH's VLM frame selection application.

filename: Aligning Moments in Time using Video Queries (2D-TAN+).pdf
title: Aligning Moments in Time using Video Queries
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Moment retrieval using video query alignment. Related baseline for Table 2.
notes: Supervised temporal grounding method.

filename: BAM-DETR Boundary-Aligned Moment Detection Transformer for Temporal Sentence Grounding in Videos.pdf
title: BAM-DETR: Boundary-Aligned Moment Detection Transformer
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised moment retrieval on QVHighlights. Competitor baseline.
notes: Boundary-aware transformer for moment detection.

filename: Boundary-Aware Self-supervised Learning for Video Scene Segmentation.pdf
title: Boundary-Aware Self-supervised Learning for Video Scene Segmentation
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Self-supervised temporal boundary detection for scene segmentation. Related to STITCH GEBD approach.
notes: Self-supervised; learns temporal boundaries without query. Adjacent to STITCH's unsupervised boundary detection.

filename: Chapter-Llama Efficient Chaptering in Hour-Long Videos with LLMs.pdf
title: Chapter-Llama: Efficient Chaptering in Hour-Long Videos with LLMs
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Video chaptering via LLM; adjacent to GEBD / temporal structure detection in long videos.
notes: Long video chaptering with LLMs; uses temporal segmentation ideas adjacent to STITCH.

filename: Dense-Captioning Events in Videos.pdf
title: Dense-Captioning Events in Videos (ActivityNet Captions)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: ActivityNet Captions is the dataset behind one of our Table 2 moment retrieval evaluation benchmarks.
notes: Dense event captioning and temporal localization dataset. Used in STITCH Table 2.

filename: EgoSchema A Diagnostic Benchmark for Very Long-form Video Language Understanding.pdf
title: EgoSchema: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Very Long-form Video Language Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long-form egocentric video QA benchmark. Related to STITCH's VLM reasoning evaluation tasks.
notes: 3min egocentric clips with QA. Adjacent to STITCH's long video reasoning evaluation.

filename: Learning 2D Temporal Adjacent Networks for Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: Learning 2D Temporal Adjacent Networks for Moment Retrieval
closest_relation_to_our_paper: 2D-TAN is a foundational moment retrieval baseline. Predecessor to more recent methods in Table 2.
notes: Supervised moment retrieval using 2D temporal map. Classic baseline.

filename: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision.pdf
title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision (CLIP)
closest_relation_to_our_paper: CLIP is a foundational vision-language model. Many methods in our comparison use CLIP features; InternVideo2 is trained on CLIP-like objectives.
notes: Core foundation model. Underpins many zero-shot / training-free video methods compared in paper.

filename: LoVR A Benchmark for Long Video Retrieval.pdf
title: LoVR: A Benchmark for Long Video Retrieval
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video retrieval benchmark. Adjacent to STITCH's moment retrieval + long video applications.
notes: Evaluates retrieval in long videos. Related to STITCH's combined moment retrieval + long video setting.

filename: Local-Global Video-Text Interactions for Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: Local-Global Video-Text Interactions for Temporal Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Supervised temporal grounding with local-global interactions. Background baseline.
notes: Predecessor temporal grounding method.

filename: Long Video Understanding with Learnable Retrieval.pdf
title: Long Video Understanding with Learnable Retrieval
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video understanding with learnable retrieval. Adjacent to STITCH's approach of reusable chunk representations.
notes: Retrieval-augmented long video understanding.

filename: LongVU Spatiotemporal Adaptive Compression for Long Video-Language Understanding.pdf
title: LongVU: Spatiotemporal Adaptive Compression for Long Video-Language Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Spatiotemporal compression for long video VLMs. STITCH evaluated as frame selection improvement over baselines like LongVU.
notes: STITCH can be used as a plug-in frame selector replacing LongVU's selection strategy.

filename: MAD A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos from Movie Audio Descriptions.pdf
title: MAD: A Scalable Dataset for Language Grounding in Videos from Movie Audio Descriptions
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Large-scale movie moment retrieval dataset. Adjacent to our moment retrieval benchmarks; potential future evaluation.
notes: Long-form temporal grounding in feature-length movies. Supporting benchmark context.

filename: Measure Twice Cut Once Citable Visual Evidence for Language Grounding.pdf
title: Measure Twice, Cut Once: Citable Visual Evidence for Language Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Temporal localization via visual evidence citation. Adjacent to STITCH's moment retrieval.
notes: Language grounding with grounded visual evidence.

filename: Modality-Aware Shot Relating and Reasoning for Video Question Answering.pdf
title: Modality-Aware Shot Relating and Reasoning for Video Question Answering
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Shot-level video QA; temporal segmentation (shot detection) used for reasoning.
notes: Shot/scene detection as a step toward video QA. Adjacent to GEBD application.

filename: Moment Sampling for Video Large Language Models.pdf
title: Moment Sampling for Video Large Language Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Frame selection/moment sampling for VLMs in long video QA. Directly related to STITCH App 3 (VLM frame selection).
notes: Training-free frame selection for VLMs. Close to STITCH's third application.

filename: MomentSeeker A Comprehensive Benchmark and A Strong Baseline For Long Video Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: MomentSeeker: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Strong Baseline for Long Video Moment Retrieval
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long video moment retrieval benchmark. Adjacent to STITCH's moment retrieval + long video setting.
notes: Comprehensive long video moment retrieval benchmark.

filename: NExT-QA NExT Phase of Question-Answering to Explaining Temporal Actions.pdf
title: NExT-QA: NExT Phase of Question-Answering to Explaining Temporal Actions
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Video QA benchmark requiring temporal reasoning. Adjacent to VLM reasoning evaluation context.
notes: Causal and temporal video QA.

filename: Point to Span Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval in Hour-Long Videos.pdf
title: Point to Span: Zero-Shot Video Moment Retrieval in Hour-Long Videos
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot moment retrieval in hour-long videos; training-free, frozen models. Direct comparison context for STITCH.
notes: Hour-long video moment retrieval without training. Close to STITCH's zero-shot Table 2 setting.

filename: Prompts to Summaries Zero-Shot Video Summarization with Vision-Language Models.pdf
title: Prompts to Summaries: Zero-Shot Video Summarization with Vision-Language Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot video summarization with VLMs. Adjacent to STITCH's training-free paradigm.
notes: Training-free summarization. Adjacent to STITCH's unsupervised chunking philosophy.

filename: Self-Chained Image-Language Model for Video Localization and Question Answering.pdf
title: Self-Chained Image-Language Model for Video Localization and Question Answering
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Multi-task frozen VLM for localization + QA. Adjacent to STITCH's general-purpose multi-task framing.
notes: Chained VLM inference for multiple video tasks.

filename: Semantic Conditioned Dynamic Modulation for Temporal Sentence Grounding in Videos.pdf
title: Semantic Conditioned Dynamic Modulation for Temporal Sentence Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Classic supervised temporal sentence grounding method. Background baseline.
notes: Foundational temporal grounding with dynamic modulation.

filename: SimBase A Simple Baseline for Temporal Video Grounding.pdf
title: SimBase: A Simple Baseline for Temporal Video Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Simple supervised baseline for temporal grounding. Comparison context.
notes: Strong simple baseline on moment retrieval benchmarks.

filename: SnAG Scalable and Accurate Video Grounding.pdf
title: SnAG: Scalable and Accurate Video Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Scalable supervised video grounding. Competitor baseline in Table 2.
notes: Strong supervised grounding baseline.

filename: TAG Boosting Text-Auditory-Visual Grounding with LLMs.pdf
title: TAG: Boosting Text-Auditory-Visual Grounding with LLMs
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot temporal grounding using LLMs + audio-visual. Adjacent to STITCH's training-free moment retrieval.
notes: Multi-modal zero-shot temporal grounding.

filename: TALL Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query.pdf
title: TALL: Temporal Activity Localization via Language Query
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Foundational temporal language grounding paper. TALL established the moment retrieval task formulation.
notes: Classic foundational moment retrieval paper.

filename: TEMPURA Object-level Semantic Context for Temporal Sentence Grounding.pdf
title: TEMPURA: Object-level Semantic Context for Temporal Sentence Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Object-level context for temporal grounding. Supervised moment retrieval.
notes: Semantic object context for grounding.

filename: TimeLoc Towards Long Horizon Temporal Localization of Atomic Actions and Events.pdf
title: TimeLoc: Towards Long-Horizon Temporal Localization of Atomic Actions and Events
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Long-horizon temporal localization; adjacent to STITCH's long video moment retrieval.
notes: Temporal localization in long videos.

filename: Towards Balanced Alignment Multi-Modal Relation Learning for Semantic Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: Towards Balanced Alignment: Multi-Modal Relation Learning for Semantic Temporal Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Multi-modal relation learning for temporal grounding. Supervised baseline.
notes: Multi-modal alignment for temporal grounding.

filename: TriDet Temporal Action Detection with Relative Boundary Modeling.pdf
title: TriDet: Temporal Action Detection with Relative Boundary Modeling
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Temporal action detection with boundary modeling. Related to GEBD boundary detection.
notes: Action detection with trident boundary modeling.

filename: UnLoc A Unified Framework for Video Localization Tasks.pdf
title: UnLoc: A Unified Framework for Video Localization Tasks
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unified temporal localization framework (moment retrieval + highlight + action detection). Adjacent to STITCH's multi-task framing.
notes: Unified framework across localization tasks; multi-task generalization philosophy aligns with STITCH.

filename: Uncovering Hidden Challenges in Query-Based Video Moment Retrieval.pdf
title: Uncovering Hidden Challenges in Query-Based Video Moment Retrieval
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Analysis of hidden biases in moment retrieval benchmarks. Relevant for understanding limitations of benchmarks used in STITCH.
notes: Reveals annotation artifacts and trivial solutions in moment retrieval datasets.

filename: UniVTG Towards Unified Video-Language Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: UniVTG: Towards Unified Video-Language Temporal Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unified VLM temporal grounding framework. Competitor baseline in Table 2.
notes: Pre-trains on multiple temporal grounding tasks and fine-tunes. Direct comparison.

filename: Vid-Morp Video Moment Retrieval Pretraining from Unlabeled Videos.pdf
title: Vid-Morp: Video Moment Retrieval Pretraining from Unlabeled Videos
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Unsupervised pretraining for moment retrieval from unlabeled videos. Adjacent to STITCH's no-annotation philosophy.
notes: Pretraining without labeled moment annotations.

filename: VideoINSTA Zero-shot Long Video Understanding via Informative Spatial-Temporal Reasoning with LLMs.pdf
title: VideoINSTA: Zero-shot Long Video Understanding via Informative Spatial-Temporal Reasoning with LLMs
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Zero-shot long video understanding via spatial-temporal reasoning with LLMs. Adjacent to STITCH VLM reasoning application.
notes: LLM-based zero-shot reasoning over long videos.

filename: VideoTree Adaptive Tree-based Video Representation for LLM Reasoning about Long Videos.pdf
title: VideoTree: Adaptive Tree-based Video Representation for LLM Reasoning about Long Videos
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Adaptive tree-based temporal structure for LLM reasoning over long videos. Adjacent to STITCH's hierarchical chunking for VLMs.
notes: Hierarchical representation for long video; close to STITCH's chunking approach for VLM frame selection.

filename: Wavelet-based Frame Selection for Efficient Video Understanding.pdf
title: Wavelet-based Frame Selection for Efficient Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Training-free frame selection via semantic boundary detection using wavelet transforms. Very closely related to STITCH's CPD-based frame selection.
notes: Unsupervised training-free frame selection; conceptually similar to STITCH's boundary-based chunking.

filename: When One Moment Isn't Enough A Multiscale Approach to Video Temporal Grounding.pdf
title: When One Moment Isn't Enough: A Multiscale Approach to Video Temporal Grounding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Multi-scale moment retrieval beyond single moments. Adjacent to STITCH's multi-granularity temporal chunks.
notes: Multi-moment grounding with hierarchical temporal scales.

filename: AdaVideoRAG Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Long-form Video QA.pdf
title: AdaVideoRAG: Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Long-form Video QA
closest_relation_to_our_paper: RAG-based long video QA with adaptive retrieval. Adjacent to STITCH's reusable chunk representations for long video.
notes: Retrieval-augmented generation for long video QA.

filename: LVC Lightweight Video Tokenization with Query Adaptive Compression.pdf
title: LVC: Lightweight Video Tokenization with Query Adaptive Compression
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Query-adaptive video compression for VLMs. Adjacent to STITCH's chunk-based frame selection.
notes: Lightweight token compression with query conditioning.

filename: STORM Efficient Stochastic Transformer based World Models for Reinforcement Learning.pdf
title: STORM: Token-Efficient Long-form Video Understanding for MLLMs
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Token-efficient long video MLLM. Adjacent to STITCH's frame selection application.
notes: Efficient token management for long video MLLMs.

filename: ChatVTG Video Temporal Grounding via Chat with Video Dialogue Large Language Models.pdf
title: ChatVTG: Video Temporal Grounding via Chat with Video Dialogue Large Language Models
closest_relation_to_our_paper: LLM chat-based temporal grounding. Peripheral to STITCH's training-free CPD approach.
notes: Uses LLM dialogue for temporal grounding; interesting but not directly compared.

filename: Deep Video Discovery Agentic Video Understanding at Scale.pdf
title: Deep Video Discovery: Agentic Video Understanding at Scale
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Agentic search for long video understanding. Tangential to STITCH's passive chunking approach.
notes: Agent-based video search at scale. Different paradigm from STITCH.

filename: DrVideo Document Retrieval Based Long Video Understanding.pdf
title: DrVideo: Document Retrieval Based Long Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: RAG-style document retrieval for long video QA. Tangential to STITCH.
notes: Uses document retrieval paradigm for long video; different from STITCH's temporal chunking.

filename: Ego4D Around the World in 3,000 Hours of Egocentric Video.pdf
title: Ego4D: Around the World in 3,000 Hours of Egocentric Video
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Large egocentric video dataset with temporal grounding tasks. Not a benchmark in STITCH but provides context for long video tasks.
notes: Peripheral; STITCH does not evaluate on Ego4D but it's a large-scale related benchmark.

filename: Hierarchical Long Video Understanding via Collaborative Multiagent Networks.pdf
title: Hierarchical Long Video Understanding via Collaborative Multiagent Networks
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Multi-agent hierarchical long video understanding. Different architectural paradigm from STITCH.
notes: Collaborative agents for long video; peripheral to STITCH's single-pass chunking.

filename: V-JEPA 2 Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning.pdf
title: V-JEPA 2: Self-Supervised Video Models Enable Understanding, Prediction and Planning
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Self-supervised video representation. Alternative backbone to InternVideo2; peripheral to STITCH's specific contribution.
notes: JEPA-based self-supervised video model. Peripheral; STITCH uses InternVideo2 as backbone.

filename: VideoMAE V2 Scaling Video Masked Autoencoders with Dual Masking.pdf
title: VideoMAE V2: Scaling Video Masked Autoencoders with Dual Masking
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Video masked autoencoder pre-training. Alternative backbone; peripheral to STITCH's specific contribution.
notes: Masked autoencoder for video pre-training. Peripheral; STITCH uses InternVideo2.

filename: Video-RAG Visually-aligned Retrieval-Augmented Long Video Comprehension.pdf
title: Video-RAG: Visually-Aligned Retrieval-Augmented Long Video Comprehension
closest_relation_to_our_paper: RAG-based long video comprehension with visual alignment. Peripheral to STITCH.
notes: Visual RAG for long video; tangential to STITCH's chunking approach.

filename: Video-XL Extra-Long Vision Language Model for Hour-Scale Video Understanding.pdf
title: Video-XL: Extra-Long Vision Language Model for Hour-Scale Video Understanding
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Extra-long video VLM for hour-scale videos. Peripheral; different approach from STITCH's training-free chunking.
notes: Long-context VLM for hour-scale video; not directly compared in STITCH.

filename: Your Interest, Your Summaries User-Query-Aware Video Summarization.pdf
title: Your Interest, Your Summaries: User-Query-Aware Video Summarization
closest_relation_to_our_paper: Query-aware video summarization. Peripheral to STITCH's unsupervised no-query chunking.
notes: User-query aware summarization; tangential to STITCH.
